question: Can the same-workgroup D512 score-page source-boundary rung become competitive by replacing the rocWMMA score/contribution fragments with CK Tile MFMA while preserving selected-KV D reuse in LDS?

code_changes.change: Added SCORE_PAGE_SCORE_CK_SHARED_KV=1. The CK score producer now stages each selected-KV D32 tile once in LDS and shares it across the four H16 score waves before advancing D, instead of each CK score wave reloading selected KV from global memory.

read.what_worked: LDS KV-D reuse fixed the earlier CK score pathology: score-only improved from 32.70 ms to 16.79 ms.
read.what_did_not_work: The CK shared-KV score path is still slower than the rocWMMA multiwave score-only path, and CK score plus CK contribution is 36.69 ms versus the rocWMMA multiwave 33.48 ms contribution-sink anchor.
read.why_this_is_not_e2e: The tested kernel is dKV-only/source-boundary diagnostic. It does not produce the full dQ+dKV backward contract and is slower than the current 11.13 ms Triton full sparse-attention backward boundary.

decision.next_kernel_shape: Move off the rocWMMA score-page bring-up CTA. Build a custom HIP/CK/MFMA source-boundary owner that preserves query-major q/dO reuse, uses explicit MFMA fragments for QK, dO.V, dQ, and dKV contribution, and adds a token-tile/group-aware dKV epilogue before global memory.
decision.specific_next_edit: Prototype the query-major token-tile no-HSK compact reducer instead of adding more SCORE_PAGE_* variants: CTA/block ownership should include several tokens and one head block so dKV groups can be reduced locally, while score/prob/scaled-dP remains consumed before HBM.

commands: docker run ... probe_sparse_mla_source_boundary_hip_coop.py --seq-len 4096 --layer-id 2 --seq-chunk 16 --selected-block 64 --block-d 512 --threads 256 --hip-define SCORE_PAGE_MODE=0 --hip-define SCORE_PAGE_MULTI_WAVE=1 --hip-define SCORE_PAGE_THREADS=256 --hip-define SCORE_PAGE_SCORE_CK=1 --hip-define SCORE_PAGE_SCORE_CK_SHARED_KV=1 --skip-correctness ...

docker run ... probe_sparse_mla_source_boundary_hip_coop.py --seq-len 4096 --layer-id 2 --seq-chunk 16 --selected-block 64 --block-d 512 --threads 256 --hip-define SCORE_PAGE_MODE=1 --hip-define SCORE_PAGE_MULTI_WAVE=1 --hip-define SCORE_PAGE_THREADS=256 --hip-define SCORE_PAGE_SCORE_CK=1 --hip-define SCORE_PAGE_SCORE_CK_SHARED_KV=1 --hip-define SCORE_PAGE_CONTRIB_CK=1 --skip-correctness ...
